results.title: On July 18, 2026, Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 became the first privately developed Indian rocket to reach orbit from Sriharikota after a 35-minute hold — the visible result of six years of reforms that opened orbital launch to private builders alongside ISRO
results.description: India's space ambitions just shifted from state monopoly to open market, with one company's successful launch signaling a fundamental reshaping of who gets to build rockets. The post On July 18, 2026, Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 became the first privately developed Indian rocket to reach orbit from Sriharikota after a 35-minute hold — the visible result of six years of reforms that opened orbital launch to private builders alongside ISRO appeared first on Space Daily .
